parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Search field type. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Sort
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to "desc".

parameters.name: SortOn
parameters.description: Field to sort on. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Single filter to apply (deprecated - use Filters instead). When set, this populates the Filters collection.

parameters.name: Filters
parameters.description: Multiple filters for advanced filtering. Can be specified multiple times to combine different filter types.

parameters.name: ExcludeGroupData
parameters.description:
Primarily for internal calls, which don't need any group data.
By having both query params we are able to allow asking for group data while explicitly dropping it by default.

get.summary: Returns matches between MySQL usersalternativenames entries and identity entities
get.description:
Queries the usersalternativenames table in MySQL and joins results with the identity table in PostgreSQL.
Only returns entries where the primaryuserid has a corresponding identity entity.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The account ID

200.description: List of matched entries including entity info, the matched identifier, and the alternative name details

post.summary: Add a single group to a single entity.
post.description:
Idempotent at the data level. Entity revision is validated up-front and the
whole request fails 409 on mismatch — same contract as the bulk endpoint.
            
## Body
```json
{ "revision": 5 }
```

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Entity id to attach the group to.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Group id to attach.

200.description: Group successfully attached. Returns the updated entity.

delete.summary: Remove a single group from a single entity.
delete.description:
Removes the entity↔group attribution row in Postgres, removes the entity's
current agents from the group in MySQL, and publishes Group_RemoveMembers
for external mirrors. Entity revision is validated up-front (409 on mismatch).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Entity id to detach the group from.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Group id to detach.

200.description: Group successfully detached. Returns the updated entity.

post.summary: Add multiple groups to a single entity.
post.description:
The entity revision is validated once up-front; a mismatch fails the whole
request with 409 and applies nothing. Each group then runs in its own
transaction — partial success is reported in MultipleResults`1
keyed by group id.
            
## Body
```json
{
  "groupIds": [9001, 9002, 9003],
  "revision": 5
}
```
            
## Limits
At most BulkLimit group ids per request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Entity id to attach groups to.

requestBody.description: Group ids and the expected entity revision.

200.description: All groups attached. Returns the result envelope.

207.description: Partial success — some groups attached, others recorded in Failed.

delete.summary: Remove multiple groups from a single entity.
delete.description:
Mirror of AddGroupsToEntity: entity revision is validated
once up-front (409 on mismatch), then each group is detached in its own
transaction. Partial success per group surfaces in the result envelope.
            
## Body
```json
{
  "groupIds": [9001, 9002],
  "revision": 5
}
```

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Entity id to detach groups from.

requestBody.description: Group ids and the expected entity revision.

post.summary: Add a single group to multiple entities.
post.description:
Each entity's revision is validated independently before the group is
attached. A revision mismatch becomes a per-entity failure (not a 409
for the whole request) — callers see exactly which entities applied and
which were rejected.
            
## Body
```json
{
  "entities": [
    { "entityId": 349, "revision": 5 },
    { "entityId": 350, "revision": 7 }
  ]
}
```
            
## Limits
At most BulkLimit entities per request.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Group id to attach to each entity.

200.description: All entities attached to the group.

207.description: Partial success — failures (including revision mismatches) listed in Failed with detail in Messages.

delete.summary: Remove a single group from multiple entities.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Group id to detach from each entity.

200.description: All entities detached from the group.

207.description: Partial success — see Failed and Messages.

get.summary: Search and retrieve identity entities with advanced filtering capabilities
get.description:
This endpoint provides comprehensive identity search functionality with support for multiple simultaneous filters.
It searches across PostgreSQL identity data and integrates with MySQL for user activity and group information.

## Multi-Filter Support
The endpoint supports applying multiple filters simultaneously using the `filters` query parameter.
Each filter can be specified multiple times to combine different filter types.

## Filter Types

### Database-Level Filters (Applied in SQL for Performance)
- **tracked**: Users who are being tracked (default behavior or explicit tracked=true)
- **untracked**: Users explicitly marked as untracked (tracked=false) 
- **nodisplayname**: Users without a display name

### MySQL-Based Filters  
- **active30days**: Users active within the last 30 days (based on MySQL lastlog data)
- **groupcount**: Users who belong to at least one group
- **nogroupcount**: Users who don't belong to any groups
- **activeusers**: Users active within a configured threshold (based on MySQL lastlog data > identity_search_active_threshold_days )
- **inactiveusers**:  Users active within a configured threshold (based on MySQL lastlog data <= identity_search_active_threshold_days )
- **unlicensedusers**: All users who do not have any user agents associated with them

### Search Types
- **all**: Search across all identifier types (default)
- **email**: Search email addresses only
- **upn**: Search User Principal Names only  
- **employeeid**: Search employee IDs only
- **alias**: Search device logon aliases
- **devicelogon**: Search device logon usernames
- **displayname**: Search display names

## Examples

### Basic Search
```
GET /identity/v1/entities?term=john.doe@company.com&type=email
```

### Multiple Filters
```  
GET /identity/v1/entities?filters=tracked&filters=active30days&filters=groupcount
```

### Pagination
```
GET /identity/v1/entities?pageSize=100&page=2
```

### Sorting
```
GET /identity/v1/entities?sortOn=displayname&sort=asc
```

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Search field type. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Sort
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to "desc".

parameters.name: SortOn
parameters.description: Field to sort on. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Single filter to apply (deprecated - use Filters instead). When set, this populates the Filters collection.

parameters.name: Filters
parameters.description: Multiple filters for advanced filtering. Can be specified multiple times to combine different filter types.

parameters.name: ExcludeGroupData
parameters.description:
Primarily for internal calls, which don't need any group data.
By having both query params we are able to allow asking for group data while explicitly dropping it by default.

500.description: Internal server error occurred during search

get.summary: Search and retrieve agent identity entities with advanced filtering capabilities
get.description:
This endpoint provides comprehensive agent identity search functionality with support for multiple simultaneous filters.
It searches across PostgreSQL identity data and integrates with MySQL for agent activity and group information.
            
## Multi-Filter Support
The endpoint supports applying multiple filters simultaneously using the `filters` query parameter.
Each filter can be specified multiple times to combine different filter types.
            
## Filter Types
            
### Database-Level Filters (Applied in SQL for Performance)
- **tracked**: Agents who are being tracked (default behavior or explicit tracked=true)
- **untracked**: Agents explicitly marked as untracked (tracked=false)
- **nodisplayname**: Agents without a display name
            
### MySQL-Based Filters
- **active30days**: Agents active within the last 30 days (based on MySQL lastlog data)
- **groupcount**: Agents who belong to at least one group
- **nogroupcount**: Agents who don't belong to any groups
            
### Search Types
- **all**: Search across all identifier types (default)
- **email**: Search email addresses only
- **upn**: Search User Principal Names only
- **employeeid**: Search employee IDs only
- **alias**: Search device logon aliases
- **devicelogon**: Search device logon usernames
- **displayname**: Search display names
            
## Examples
            
### Basic Search
```
GET /identity/v1/agents?term=john.doe@company.com&type=email
```
            
### Multiple Filters
```
GET /identity/v1/agents?filters=tracked&filters=active30days&filters=groupcount
```
            
### Pagination
```
GET /identity/v1/agents?pageSize=100&page=2
```
            
### Sorting
```
GET /identity/v1/agents?sortOn=displayname&sort=asc
```

post.summary: Add one or more Do Not Track (DNT) rules.
post.description:
Creates new DNT rules to prevent specific users or logon domains from being tracked.
            
## Request Body
- **rules**: Array of DNT rule objects, each containing:
  - `username`: The username or alias to stop tracking.
  - `domain`: The associated domain. 
  - `anyDomain`: Flag to match any domain for the username.
            
## Validation
- The maximum number of rules per request is **50**.
- Returns **400 Bad Request** if the limit is exceeded or if rules are missing.
- Validation checks for conflicts with existing tracked identities, including:
  - Direct matches (username + domain in identity records).
  - Alternative name matches (username + domain in usersalternativenames table from merged agents).
- When a conflict is detected, the response includes optional `entityId` and `revision` fields in the failure object.
- If a conflict includes `entityId` and `revision`, the UI should use the bulk DNT endpoint to untrack the associated user.
            
## Example
```json
{
  "rules": [
    { "username": "jdoe", "domain": "bgrove.com", "anyDomain": false }
  ]
}
```

requestBody.description: The DNT creation request containing one or more rules.

200.description: Returns a  containing details of created, duplicate, and failed rules.

400.description: The request was invalid or exceeded the 50-rule limit.

put.summary: Update existing Do Not Track (DNT) rules.
put.description:
Allows modifying existing DNT entries, such as changing domains or flags.
            
## Request Body
- **rules**: Array of DNT rule objects, each containing:
  - `username`: Username to update (required).
  - `domain`: Domain to update (required, use null for anyDomain).
  - `anyDomain`: Boolean flag indicating if rule applies to all domains (required).
  - `id`: Rule ID to update (required).
- **Note**: All fields must be provided for each rule.

## Validation
- The maximum number of rules per request is **50**.
- Returns **400 Bad Request** if the rule ID does not exist or exceeds request limits.
- Validation checks for conflicts with existing tracked identities, including:
  - Direct matches (username + domain in identity records).
  - Alternative name matches (username + domain in usersalternativenames table from merged agents).
- When a conflict is detected, the response includes optional `entityId` and `revision` fields in the failure object.
- If a conflict includes `entityId` and `revision`, the UI should use the bulk DNT endpoint to untrack the associated user.
            
## Example
```json
{
  "rules": [
    { "id": 124, "username": "jdoe", "domain": "bgrove.net", "anyDomain": false }
  ]
}
```

requestBody.description: The DNT PUT request containing updated rule data with all required fields.

200.description: Returns a  containing per-rule update results.

400.description: The request was invalid, contained missing rule IDs, or exceeded the 50-rule limit.

delete.summary: Delete one or more Do Not Track (DNT) rules.
delete.description:
Permanently removes DNT entries matching the provided rule IDs.
            
## Request Body
- **ids**: Array of rule IDs to delete.
            
## Validation
- The maximum number of IDs per request is **50**.
- Returns **400 Bad Request** if IDs validation fails.
            
## Example
```json
{
  "ids": [124, 125, 126]
}
```

requestBody.description: The DNT deletion request containing one or more rule IDs to remove.

200.description: Returns a  with lists of successful and failed deletions only.

get.summary: Retrieve all Do Not Track (DNT) entries for the current account.

parameters.name: Term
parameters.description:
When set (non-empty after trim), matches username or logon domain (and for identity rows, display id
and associated user email values) with a single substring search; takes precedence over
Username and Domain.

200.description: Returns a  with results from both Identity and MySQL sources.

400.description: The request was invalid or contained unsupported filter/sort parameters.

post.summary: Add Do Not Track rules from a CSV file.
post.description:
Multipart form with field file: UTF-8 CSV with header row. Required columns: username, domain (case-insensitive).
Use a single * in domain for any-domain. Empty domain is invalid.
At most 100 data rows and file size at most 1 MB.

200.description: Returns a  for each row (successes, failures, duplicates).

400.description: Invalid multipart request, missing file, bad CSV headers, empty rows, over row limit, or file too large.

post.summary: Merge multiple source entities into a single target entity with optimistic concurrency control
post.description:
This endpoint consolidates multiple identity entities into a single target entity, combining all identifiers
(emails, UPNs, employee IDs, etc.) from the source entities into the target. Source entities are deleted
after the merge completes successfully.
            
## Merge Behavior
            
### Identity Consolidation
- All identifiers from source entities are transferred to the target entity
- Duplicate identifiers are automatically handled
- The target entity remains; source entities are deleted
- The target entity's revision is incremented once after all merges complete
            
### Agent Merging (Optional)
When `merges` is provided with agent user IDs (first ID is the target/primary agent):
- The specified agents are merged together with the first agent as the target.
- Agent activity history is preserved and associated with the target entity
- Downstream systems are notified of the agent reassignments
            
## Examples
            
### Basic Entity Merge
```json
POST /identity/v1/entities/merge
{
  "target": {
    "entityId": 100,
    "revision": 5
  },
  "sources": [
    {
      "entityId": 101,
      "revision": 3
    },
    {
      "entityId": 102,
      "revision": 7
    }
  ],
}
```
            
### Merge with Agent Transfer
```json
POST /identity/v1/entities/merge
{
  "target": {
    "entityId": 200,
    "revision": 10
  },
  "sources": [
    {
      "entityId": 201,
      "revision": 8
    }
  ],
  "merges": [42, 55, 63]
}
```
            
## Validation Rules
- At least one source entity must be provided
- An entity cannot be merged into itself (target cannot be in sources)
- All revisions must match current entity states

requestBody.description: Merge request containing target entity, source entities, and merge options

200.description: Successfully merged entities. Returns the updated target entity.

500.description: Internal server error occurred during merge operation

post.summary: Reassign agents to entities then merge them via the admin API
post.description:
For each source, if the specified agent is not already on the specified entity it is reassigned there first.
After all reassignments, the agents in the Merges list are merged together via the admin API.
Merges[0] is the primary agent that survives. No entity-level merge is performed.
            
## Example Request
```json
POST /identity/v1/agents/reassignthenmerge
{
  "target": { "entityId": 100, "revision": 5 },
  "sources": [
    { "entityId": 101, "revision": 3, "userId": 42 },
    { "entityId": 102, "revision": 7, "userId": 55 }
  ],
  "merges": [42, 55]
}
```
            
## Validation Rules
- At least one source must be provided
- Target entity cannot be in sources
- Merges list must be non-empty; Merges[0] is the primary (surviving) agent
- No duplicate userIds in sources
- All agent userIds in Merges must exist across the target or source entities

requestBody.description: Request containing target entity, source agents with their designated entities, and the agent merge order

200.description: Successfully reassigned and merged agents. Returns the target entity.

post.summary: Search for identity entities using multiple identifier types
post.description:
This endpoint searches for entities by any combination of identifiers using OR logic.
An entity matches if it has ANY of the provided identifiers across all types.
Account ID is resolved from the authenticated user's JWT token.
            
## Example Request
```json
{
  "emailAddresses": ["john.doe@company.com", "jane.smith@company.com"],
  "employeeIds": ["EMP001", "EMP002"],
  "upns": ["john@domain.com"],
  "deviceLogons": [{"user": "jdoe", "logonDomain": "CORP"}],
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "displayName": "John Doe"
}
```
            
## Matching Logic
- Uses OR logic across all identifier types
- An entity is returned if it matches ANY email OR ANY employee ID OR ANY UPN, etc.
- Results are deduplicated (same entity won't appear multiple times)
- Name matching requires both FirstName AND LastName to be provided together
            
## Response
Returns full IdentityDetailsResponse objects including:
- All identifiers (emails, UPNs, employee IDs, device logons, names)
- Associated agents with activity data
- Group memberships and primary group
- Tracking status and entity status (active/inactive/unlicensed)
- Entity revision for optimistic concurrency control

200.description: Successfully found matching entities (may be empty if no matches)

400.description: Invalid request (no identifiers provided or exceeds limit)

put.summary: Bulk update clients with license approval state
put.description:
Applies a single license approval state to all specified clients.
            
## Request Body
- **licenseApproved**: License approval state to apply to all clients (true = approve, false = reject).
- **clients**: Array of integer client IDs to update.
            
## Example Request
```json
{
  "licenseApproved": true,
  "clients": [101, 202, 303]
}
```
            
## Example Response
```json
{
  "succeeded": [101, 202],
  "failed": [303],
  "succeededCount": 2,
  "failedCount": 1,
  "messages": ["Client 303 not found"]
}
```

requestBody.description: The bulk update request containing a license approval state and the list of client IDs to update.

200.description: Returns a  with succeeded and failed client ID lists.

post.summary: Internal endpoint to search for identity entities using multiple identifier types
post.description:
This endpoint searches for entities by any combination of identifiers using OR logic.
Account ID is provided in the URL path.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Account ID from URL path

200.description: List of matching identity entities with full details

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Search field type. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Sort
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to "desc".

parameters.name: SortOn
parameters.description: Field to sort on. Defaults to "all".

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Single filter to apply (deprecated - use Filters instead). When set, this populates the Filters collection.

parameters.name: Filters
parameters.description: Multiple filters for advanced filtering. Can be specified multiple times to combine different filter types.

parameters.name: ExcludeGroupData
parameters.description:
Primarily for internal calls, which don't need any group data.
By having both query params we are able to allow asking for group data while explicitly dropping it by default.

post.summary:
Bulk entity actions for the account: same semantics as POST /identity/v1/entities/bulk, but callers send
entity ids only and this internal path bypasses client revision validation.

IdentityDetailsResponse.description: Detailed information about a single identity entity, including all associated identifiers, agents, and metadata

agents.description: List of active agents associated with this identity

emails.description: Email addresses associated with this identity

groups.description: Groups that this identity belongs to

primaryGroupId.description: ID of the primary group for this identity

primaryGroupName.description: Name of the primary group for this identity

upns.description: User Principal Names (UPNs) associated with this identity

employeeId.description: The primary employee ID for this identity (first in the list)

employeeIds.description: All employee IDs associated with this identity

id.description: Unique identifier for this identity entity

revision.description: Current revision number for optimistic concurrency control

deviceLogons.description: Device logon credentials (username@domain combinations) associated with this identity

deletedAgents.description: Agents that were previously associated with this identity but have been deleted

displayName.description: Display name for this identity (typically the user's full name)

timezone.description: Timezone identifier for this identity (IANA timezone identifier, e.g., "America/Chicago")

tracked.description: Whether this identity is being tracked for monitoring and analytics

status.description: Status of the identity based on agent activity

created.description: When this identity was first created

updated.description: When this identity was last updated

displayId.description: The primary identifier used to display this identity in the UI

licenseStatus.description:
License status derived from the agent's delete_date.
"approved" = no delete_date (active agent),
"pending" = delete_date is 9999-12-31 23:59:59,
"rejected" = delete_date is 0001-01-01 00:00:00,
"deleted" = any other non-null delete_date.

EntityStatus.description: Entity status based on agent presence and activity

SearchType.description: Search and sort field types for identity queries

FilterType.description: Supported filter values for the Filters property

EntitySnapshot.description:
Represents reconstructed entity state at a point in time,
built purely from change log replay.

ChangeSource.description: Source system that originated identity data changes

ChangeLogEntry.description:
Lightweight metadata about a change log entry (no data payload).
Used by the entity revision viewer to list and navigate changes.

UserAlternativeNameMisMatch.description: Represents a mismatch between a MySQL usersalternativenames entry and an identity entity

entityId.description: The identity entity ID in PostgreSQL

identifier.description: The identifier value in the identity table that matched

identifierType.description: The identifier type from the identity table (e.g. upn, email, displayname)

EntityRevisionRequest.description:
Request payload for singular entity-mutation endpoints whose entity id
lives in the URL — carries just the revision the caller expects. A
mismatch fails the request with 409.

MultipleResultsOfInteger.description:
Bulk-operation result: which keys succeeded, which failed, and any informational
messages. Mirrors grand_central's ActivTrak.Common.Util.Web.Models.MultipleResults
without taking a project reference.

GroupIdsRequest.description:
Request payload for entity-keyed endpoints: a list of group ids and the
single entity revision the caller expects. A revision mismatch fails the
whole request with 409.

EntityIdsRequest.description:
Request payload for group-keyed endpoints: a list of entity ids each paired
with the revision the caller expects the entity to be at. Revisions are
checked per-entity; mismatches surface as per-entity failures rather than
failing the whole request.

EntityRevisionPair.description:
(EntityId, Revision) tuple used by requests that operate on multiple
entities. Each entity is validated against its own revision before the
operation is applied.

FieldEditsDto.description: Data transfer object for field edits in API requests

source.description: Source system that originated this identity data change (numeric value)

FieldDto.description: Data transfer object for field values in API requests

DeviceLogonDto.description:
Data transfer object for device logon values in API requests.
Device logons represent username and domain combinations for Windows authentication.

user.description: The username component of the device logon

logonDomain.description: The domain component of the device logon (optional)

id.description: Old value identifier for update/delete operations with structured User and LogonDomain properties.

source.description: Source of the devicelogon data. Only used for backwards compatibility with legacy format.

BulkFieldEditsRequest.description: Request model for bulk field edits operations

MergeEntitiesRequest.description: Request model for merging multiple entities into a single target

target.description: The entity that will receive all identifiers from the sources (this entity will remain)

sources.description: The entities that will be merged into the target (these entities will be deleted)

merges.description:
Agent user IDs to merge, with the first ID being the target (primary) agent.
When null or empty, no agent merge is performed.

MergeEntity.description:
Represents an entity participating in a merge operation.
Contains entity ID and revision for optimistic concurrency control.

revision.description: The entity revision for optimistic concurrency control.

ReassignThenMergeRequest.description: Request model for reassigning agents to entities and merging them together via the admin API.

target.description: The target entity for revision validation.

sources.description:
The source agents to reassign. Each source specifies the entity the agent is on
before the merge is performed.

merges.description:
Agent userIds to merge. Merges[0] is the primary agent that survives the merge.
All other agents are merged into it via the admin API.

ReassignThenMergeSource.description: A source agent participating in the reassign-then-merge operation.

entityId.description: The entity ID where this agent should end up after reassignment.

revision.description: The current revision of the entity for optimistic concurrency control.

userId.description: The agent userId to reassign to this entity.

IdentityLookupResponse.description: Response model for multi-identifier identity search

results.description: List of matching identity entities with full details

IdentityLookupRequest.description: Request model for searching identities using multiple identifier types

emailAddresses.description: Email addresses to search for (OR logic with other identifiers)

employeeIds.description: Employee IDs to search for (OR logic with other identifiers)

upns.description: User Principal Names to search for (OR logic with other identifiers)

deviceLogons.description: Device logon credentials to search for (OR logic with other identifiers)

displayName.description: Display name to search for (OR logic with other identifiers)

DeviceLogonSearch.description: Device logon search criteria with username and optional domain

user.description: The username component of the device logon

logonDomain.description: The domain component of the device logon (optional)

succeeded.description: List of client IDs successfully updated

failed.description: List of client IDs that failed to update

licenseApproved.description: License approval state to apply to all clients (true = approve, false = reject, null = no change)

clients.description: List of client IDs to update

InternalIdentityBulkActionRequest.description:
Internal bulk entity actions: same IdentityBulkActionType set as IdentityBulkActionRequest,
but callers send entity ids only and the internal path bypasses revision validation.
